data.title: US designates four groups in Europe as terrorists, calls them 'violent Antifa'
data.description:
WASHINGTON (Reuters) -The United States on Thursday designated four groups in Germany, Italy and Greece as global terrorists, accusing them of being "violent antifa groups" as President Donald Trump takes aim at left-wing groups.

U.S. Secretary of State Marco Rubio in a statement said he had designated German-based Antifa Ost as "Specially Designated Global Terrorists," along with three other groups in Greece and Italy, in an effort to build on Trump's "commitment to confront Antifa's campaign of political violence."

Rubio said he intends to also designate the groups as "Foreign Terrorist Organizations" from November 20 and warned that Washington would target other groups around the world.

"Groups affiliated with this movement ascribe to revolutionary anarchist or Marxist ideologies, including anti-Americanism, 'anti-capitalism,' and anti-Christianity, using these to incite and justify violent assaults domestically and overseas," Rubio said.

"The United States will continue using all available tools to protect our national security and public safety and will deny funding and resources to terrorists, including targeting other Antifa groups across the globe."

Trump and his Republican allies have accused antifa followers of fomenting political violence following the September assassination of conservative activist Charlie Kirk and amid protests against federal immigration authorities in cities including Los Angeles, Chicago and Portland, Oregon.

Trump threatened to pursue antifa during his 2017-2021 term in office, and in September called antifa a terrorist organization in an executive order. Some national security law experts said the designation was legally questionable because antifa, short for anti-fascist, has no official leadership or organizational structure.

(Reporting by Daphne Psaledakis, Maiya Keidan and Christian Martinez; editing by Susan Heavey and Leslie Adler)

Standard Chartered PLC Announces Group Company Secretary Changes, Effective 14 November 2025
data.description: Standard Chartered PLC (Company) announced that Adrian de Souza, Group Company Secretary will leave his role on 14 November 2025 with Scott Corrigan taking on that position from 14 November 2025, in addition to his role as Group General Counsel. Mr. Corrigan, a qualified lawyer, joined the Company in 2014 and has held the role of Group General Counsel since January 2025.

Lloyds Announces Stepdown of Jo Harris as CEO of the Group?s Mass Affluent Proposition
data.description:
Quilter is shaping its executive team for the new year with two senior appointments to the committee. Jo Harris will join the firm in January 2026 in the newly created role of chief customer officer, where she will be tasked with evolving Quilter?s proposition using technology and automation. Harris joins the firm following an 11-year career at Lloyds Bank, having stepped down from her position as CEO of the group?s mass affluent proposition in June.

Before this, she was head of telephone and internet banking at Royal Bank of Scotland.

data.title: NBCC Limited Reports Earnings Results for the Second Quarter and Six Months Ended September 30, 2025
data.description:
NBCC (India) Limited reported earnings results for the second quarter and six months ended September 30, 2025. For the second quarter, the company reported sales was INR 29,101.98 million compared to INR 24,457.26 million a year ago. Revenue was INR 30,171.57 million compared to INR 25,129.59 million a year ago. Net income was INR 1,535.16 million compared to INR 1,221.17 million a year ago. Basic earnings per share from continuing operations was INR 0.57 compared to INR 0.45 a year ago. Diluted earnings per share from continuing operations was INR 0.57 compared to INR 0.45 a year ago.
For the six months, sales was INR 53,013.84 million compared to INR 45,882.61 million a year ago. Revenue was INR 54,826.47 million compared to INR 47,091.68 million a year ago. Net income was INR 2,856.46 million compared to INR 2,267.38 million a year ago. Basic earnings per share from continuing operations was INR 1.06 compared to INR 0.84 a year ago. Diluted earnings per share from continuing operations was INR 1.06 compared to INR 0.84 a year ago.

data.description:

Fabric8Labs, Inc. announced that it has received $50 million in funding from a group of investors
data.description: Fabric8Labs, Inc. announced that it has received $50 million in a round of funding led by returning investors New Enterprise Associates, Inc. and Intel Capital Corporation on November 13, 2025. The company has issued Convertible Preferred Stock in the transaction. The transaction included participation from new investors SK hynix Inc., Marunouchi Innovation Partners Co., Ltd., Ericsson Ventures, Masco Ventures, a fund managed by, Masco Corporation, Investment Arm, Toppan Global Venture Partners, Inc. and returning investors Lam Research Capital, TDK Ventures, Inc. and Schneider Electric Ventures.

data.title: Los Angeles Community College District to Offer $300 Million in Bonds for Repairs, Modernization
data.description:
By Patrick Sheridan


The Los Angeles Community College District plans to sell $300 million in bonds for the modernization and equipping of classroom facilities.

The tax-exempt Series F 2016 Election General Obligation Bonds have maturities ranging from 2026 to 2031. Pricing is scheduled for Nov. 20 with closing slated for Dec. 16, as per the roadshow document accompanying the preliminary official statement posted Wednesday on MuniOS.

Interest on the bonds is payable semiannually on Feb. 1 and Aug. 1 of each year, commencing Feb. 1, 2026.

The bonds are general obligation bonds approved by voters within the district and are payable solely from ad valorem property taxes levied annually during the period the bonds are outstanding.

The bond measure called for upgrading security and technology at the district's colleges, as well as repairing or replacing outdated classrooms, science labs and critical infrastructure. Projects include the removal of lead paint and asbestos, providing handicapped accessibility, improving earthquake safety and replacing outdated electrical wiring, according to the Build LACCD website.

The Los Angeles Community College District encompasses an area covering more than 882 square miles including the City of Los Angeles and 39 neighboring cities and communities. The district serves approximately 216,862 students annually at nine accredited colleges, according to the roadshow document.

S&P Global Ratings and Moody's Ratings have issued AA+ and Aaa ratings on the bonds, respectively.

Joint senior managers on the offering are BofA Securities and Ramirez & Co.


Write to Patrick Sheridan at patrick.sheridan@wsj.com


(END) Dow Jones Newswires

11-13-25 1307ET

data.title: US 30-Year Auction High Yield Falls From Previous Month, Demand Lower
Monte dei Paschi Successfully Places EUR500 Million Green Bond
data.description:
(Alliance News) - Banca Monte dei Paschi di Siena Spa announced on Thursday that it has successfully completed the placement of its first green senior preferred unsecured bond, raising a total of EUR500 million.

The bond, which carries an annual fixed interest rate of 3.25%, has a maturity of six years and three months, with a scheduled redemption in February 2032 and an early call option after five years.

The offering attracted demand of approximately EUR2 billion--four times the available amount--demonstrating strong interest from both Italian and international investors. The re-offer price was set at 99.647%, with a spread of 90 basis points above the mid-swap, 30 basis points lower than the initial guidance and 40 basis points below the most recent similar issue in May.

Geographically, orders were distributed with Italy accounting for 36%, followed by Germany, Austria, and Switzerland at 17%, France at 15%, Southern Europe at 13%, and other countries at 19%. A significant share also came from investors with ESG-focused strategies.

The issuance forms part of MPS's 2025 Funding Plan and its 2024 Green, Social and Sustainability Bond Framework, with proceeds earmarked for sustainable environmental initiatives.

The bond will be listed on the Luxembourg Stock Exchange, with expected ratings of 'Baa3' from Moody's, 'BBB-' from Fitch, and 'BBB' from Morningstar DBRS. The placement was managed by BBVA, Deutsche Bank, HSBC, JPMorgan, Mediobanca, Natixis, and NatWest.

MPS shares closed Thursday up 2.0% at EUR8.79 each.

By Giuseppe Fabio Ciccomascolo, Alliance News senior reporter

Comments and questions: redazione@alliancenews.com
